81a8820fbb17.action: Add a brief summary step at the end of the nightly workflow that prints a structured log line — e.g. 'Run complete: X suggestions fetched, Y new, Z dismissed, N parse errors' — and fails the job if parse errors exceed zero.

36f01aa8410d.action: Pre-generate the sitemap at build time using `generateSitemaps` or a build script that writes a static `public/sitemap.xml`, rather than serving it via a runtime API route. Seed it with known static paths (home, league pages) so it is never empty on a cold deploy.

b1d3409d1502.action: Resolve the persistent ~1347ms initial response time by verifying the homepage ISR cache is actually being hit at Vercel's edge. Check the 'x-vercel-cache' response header on the live site — if it reads MISS or BYPASS instead of HIT on repeat requests, the static/ISR output is not being served from edge cache. Confirm that no dynamic per-request logic (e.g. reading auth cookies, calling getServerSideProps, or middleware rewriting the homepage route) is forcing a full server render and invalidating the cache. The recent 'perf: cut initial response time' commit addressed this but the live response time of 1347ms suggests it has not fully resolved — the open issue tracks investigation but a concrete next step is to curl -I https://thatswhoi.am/ twice in succession and confirm the second request returns x-vercel-cache: HIT.

e30275a62977.action: The open SEO/Domain issue notes that thatswhoi.am is not yet configured as the primary domain in Vercel and no 301 redirect exists from onepagecv-theta.vercel.app to thatswhoi.am. Until this is done, Google will index both origins separately, splitting PageRank and potentially causing duplicate-content penalties. Add the canonical domain in the Vercel project dashboard under 'Domains', set onepagecv-theta.vercel.app to redirect 301 to https://thatswhoi.am, and update the canonical meta tag value (already present per recent commits) to use the thatswhoi.am URL explicitly. The og:image absolute URL should also be updated to the canonical domain so social crawlers do not follow a redirect chain when fetching the preview image.

86208f4a6ceb.action: Replace the logo fallback `/logo.png` used as `og:image` on static pages with a properly sized 1200×630 Open Graph image. The MONITOR.md explicitly flags this as a known gap. A correctly sized OG image also removes any potential CLS or render-blocking penalty that social crawlers and link-preview bots incur when they fetch and scale an unoptimised logo asset.

7cb1ac45d6f3.action: Audit all `<Image>` usages in the play catalogue and member grid to confirm every image has an explicit `width`, `height`, and a meaningful `sizes` attribute matching its rendered breakpoints. Also verify that hero or above-the-fold images use `priority={true}` to trigger `<link rel=preload>` in the HTML, eliminating LCP-blocking network round trips.

7a35ea74a106.action: Replace the heuristic performance score in `site.js` with a real Lighthouse CI run integrated into the nightly GitHub Actions workflow using `treosh/lighthouse-ci-action`. Output the Core Web Vitals scores (LCP, CLS, TBT) as structured JSON artifacts so the monitor agent can consume real numbers instead of estimates.

43972390a3ad.action: Audit and harden the admin match-entry API routes to confirm they enforce authentication server-side on every request. Specifically, verify that all Next.js API routes or Server Actions that write match data (add, edit, recalculate stats) call `getServerSession` (or equivalent NextAuth server-side check) and return a 401/403 immediately if the session is absent or not admin — not just relying on the UI hiding the form.

2bb1dccbd439.action: Add server-side input validation to the match-entry form handler (API route or Server Action) using a schema validation library such as Zod. eb30d8f18852.action: Add a Content Security Policy (CSP) header and the supporting security headers (X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy) via the `headers()` block in next.config.js. At minimum, set a strict-origin-when-cross-origin Referrer-Policy, DENY or SAMEORIGIN for X-Frame-Options, and a CSP that whitelists only known script/style/connect sources (Vercel Analytics endpoint, your own origin, any AI API host). Test with https://securityheaders.com after deploying.

19331dd58c37.action: Audit every server action and API route that accepts user-supplied string input (CV text fields, profile fields, any free-text prompt passed to the AI) and add explicit length caps and basic sanitisation before the data is persisted or forwarded. Concretely: define a shared Zod schema with `.max()` constraints that mirrors your DB column limits, apply it at the route boundary, and return a structured 400 with a safe error message on violation. This is separate from the existing Upstash rate-limiting which only throttles frequency, not payload content.

0c0588b00211.action: On play pages that have an upcoming date with no tickets_url yet (e.g., the October 2026 show), replace any blank or hidden ticket CTA with an explicit disabled button or inline note such as 'Biglietti disponibili a breve' instead of omitting the action entirely. The button should be visually present but non-interactive, so users know ticketing is coming rather than wondering whether the show is bookable.

1626d29fde09.action: Add a clear, human-readable explanation to the daily email when the '[Analysis] Could not parse suggestions' error occurs — instead of silently failing or showing a raw error label, include a sentence like 'The AI response couldn't be parsed tonight. No suggestions to show — it will retry tomorrow.' so the reader knows the system is alive but had a hiccup.

5cd9fa3c590b.action: Add a small confirmation signal to the one-click dismiss flow — for example, the dismiss URL response page (served by the Vercel endpoint) should render a minimal success message like 'Got it — this suggestion has been dismissed and won't appear again' rather than a raw JSON response or blank page, so the user knows the action registered.

275301cce224.action: Investigate and fix the known issue where the sitemap is empty at build time due to runtime API calls failing during the Vercel build phase. Switch sitemap generation to use direct database queries (via the Vercel Postgres client) rather than internal HTTP API calls, so the sitemap is fully populated in the static output at deploy time rather than only after the app is live.

e4116d222cd9.action: Investigate why the homepage response time is 887ms on a Vercel-hosted Next.js app and confirm ISR/edge caching is actually being hit. Check the response headers for 'x-vercel-cache: HIT' — if it shows MISS or BYPASS on repeat requests, the page is falling back to a full server render every time. Fix by ensuring the page exports 'revalidate' correctly and is not opting out of caching via dynamic cookies/headers reads at the top level.

750887bbc24e.action: Configure the canonical URL to point to thatswhoi.am and set up a 301 redirect from onepagecv-theta.vercel.app to it in Vercel project settings. Until this is done, Google may index both origins separately, splitting any link equity and causing duplicate-content penalties that suppress ranking for both URLs.

89fb7bc3664a.action: Investigate the response time regression from 1617ms to 2832ms observed in tonight's live site check — a 75% increase over the already-flagged baseline. Specifically: (1) run `next build` locally and compare ISR revalidation logs to identify which routes are being re-fetched on cold hits, (2) check Vercel Function logs for the homepage and any play-catalogue routes to see if `unstable_cache` TTLs have expired or been bypassed, (3) confirm that the `revalidate` value on ISR routes is not set too aggressively short (e.g. under 60s), which would cause near-constant regeneration under low traffic, and (4) verify no new unoptimized Supabase query was introduced in the last deploy cycle even though no commits landed in 7 days (check for a deploy triggered by a dependency update or Vercel config change).

c1d192f07d15.action: Replace the `/logo.png` fallback used as `og:image` on static pages with a purpose-built 1200×630 PNG/WebP share image, and serve it via Vercel's built-in CDN with a long `Cache-Control` header (`public, max-age=31536000, immutable`). The image should be placed in `/public/og-default.png`, referenced in the root `layout.tsx` metadata object, and compressed to under 200 KB (use Squoosh or `sharp` in a one-off build script). This is already listed on the roadmap but has a direct performance angle: the current logo file is almost certainly not sized or compressed for social crawlers, causing slower unfurl times and a missing opportunity to control how the site appears when shared — which affects click-through and thus organic discoverability.

9066e23292d3.action: Replace the heuristic performance score in `site.js` with a real Lighthouse CI run integrated into the GitHub Actions workflow, even on a scheduled weekly basis rather than nightly.
